mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals impending recession risk, causing investors to compress equity risk premiums and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC > 0.05 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts combined with rising consumer credit indicate robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Positive SPX returns do not correlate with simultaneous HOUST and RSAFS increases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects rising capacity utilization and operational leverage, which directly boosts corporate margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows no predictive power for 5-day returns in early-cycle regimes, or forward returns are flat across deciles of INDPRO momentum.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation persisting alongside accommodative policy erodes real yields and triggers volatility, depressing short-term equity returns as central banks face forced tightening.
failure_condition: SPX returns remain positive when inflation exceeds threshold despite low rates, or the joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth directly scales forward earnings expectations, driving positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon as corporate profits adjust to macroeconomic fundamentals.
failure_condition: GDP growth shows negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward returns, or rolling Spearman correlation remains below 0.10 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained strong payroll growth signals economic resilience, boosting risk appetite and equity valuations as investors price in lower recession probabilities and higher dividend growth.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are flat or negative despite sustained job growth, or the signal generates a negative information coefficient in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Coordinated strength in housing construction and industrial output indicates a synchronized supply-demand balance, maximizing equity risk premium and driving positive short-term market returns.
failure_condition: SPX returns decline when both housing and industrial indicators exceed thresholds simultaneously, or the interaction effect reverses sign in out-of-sample periods.
